explanation: This command uses path normalization (double backslash escaping) to bypass the Sigma rule that detects '.sys' followed by a comma or space. The rule may not capture this variation due to the escaped path syntax.

This command uses path normalization with a trailing backslash and a dot (\.) to obfuscate the .sys file extension. This may bypass the rule by altering the string pattern while maintaining valid Windows path syntax.

This command uses an insertion of a quote after the .sys file to alter the string pattern. The Sigma rule may not detect this variation if it does not account for quote placement around the file name.
This is the original true positive command. It is included to show the baseline for comparison with the mutated versions.
